rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes when describing interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and named constants to define bounds in assumptions and guarantees.
Structure AGREE equations and constants within component type annexes for interface contracts.
Encode boolean logic using standard operators in AGREE expressions.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contracts.
Component type contracts should use `const name : type = value;` and `eq name : type = expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific assign clauses in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL timing properties as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their scope in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
